political.s: F45 Training is a private fitness franchisor headquartered in Austin, Texas. No significant federal political donations or lobbying activity found in available public records. Company focuses on business expansion and franchise growth rather than political engagement.

charity.s: F45 Training has established limited philanthropic initiatives including the Veterans Impact Program 45 (VIP45) to mentor veterans and active-duty personnel. Company also partners with cancer charities globally and provides corporate wellness programs, though overall charitable giving amounts are not publicly detailed.
charity.focus: Veterans support, cancer charities, corporate wellness

environment.s: F45 Training lacks comprehensive public environmental sustainability reporting. Company operates 1,500+ studios globally with significant physical infrastructure footprint. Limited public commitment to carbon reduction, energy efficiency, or sustainability standards evident in available materials.

labor.s: F45 Training operates as a franchise model with varied employment conditions across franchisee-operated studios. Average employee salary approximately $57,787 annually with significant variation by role and location. No major OSHA violations or NLRB actions identified, though labor data for franchise employees is decentralized.

dei.s: F45 Training has demonstrated pro-DEI initiatives including establishment of Veterans Impact Program 45 and participation in fitness industry DEI committees. Company includes diverse fitness professionals on leadership and advocacy platforms, though formal DEI policy documentation is limited.

privacy.s: F45 Training collects member fitness data through apps and wearables including biometric information. Limited public privacy policy detail available, though company partners with technology vendors like Samsung for data collection.

execPay.s: F45 Training paid CEO Tom Dowd $1 million annually as of 2022, with Mark Wahlberg taking $1 salary as chief brand officer. Average employee compensation approximately $57,787 annually, creating estimated CEO-to-worker pay ratio of approximately 17:1.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
